title: Canzoneri 2013 tool-use PPS reshaping
citation: Canzoneri, E., Ubaldi, S., Rastelli, V., Finisguerra, A., Bassolino, M., & Serino, A. (2013). Tool-use reshapes the boundaries of body and peripersonal space representations. Experimental Brain Research, 228, 25-42.
notes: Tool-use PPS plasticity profile. The publication reports an audio-tactile interaction task before and after use of a 1 m tool. This preload captures the canonical Canzoneri-style PPS measurement scaffold; tool-use versus control-training session structure remains metadata until the GUI supports ordered intervention phases.

reference_parameters.reported_intervention: 1 m tool-use training before/after PPS measurement
reference_parameters.baseline_subtype: tactile-only timing controls during silence, exact table not verified from accessible source
reference_parameters.response_rule: speeded response to tactile target, ignore sound
implementation_gaps: exact trial count and ITI table from full paper

provenance.source: Repository/source abstract reports PPS measured with an audio-tactile interaction task before and after using a 1 m tool.
Profile inherits the Canzoneri-family dynamic sound timing pending full-method verification.
